Alessia Hero Review - Empires and Puzzles Strategy Guide

Is Alessia still the queen of 3-star Bloody Battle tournaments? We break down her Bewitched Daggers, analyze her fast-speed max health reduction mechanics, and see if her stats can survive the 2026 power creep in this detailed strategy guide.
Alessia Review: The Baby Horus of 3-Star Tournaments
Alessia: The Masked Health-Sapper of the Carnival
Alessia is a Rare (3-star) Nature hero from the

event. When she burst onto the scene in mid-2023, she was immediately hailed as a "Baby Horus" due to her ability to slash Max Health at Fast speed. As a Rogue, she brings the annoying Dodge mechanic to the table, making her a slippery nuisance in tournaments. While she doesn't have a costume yet, her base form remains a unique tool in the 3-star ecosystem, particularly for those who enjoy watching their opponents' health bars permanently shrink. She’s like a dentist who works very, very quickly—and takes your teeth with her.

Alessia embraces the masquerade theme with flair. She sports a green feathery mask that looks like it was plucked from a festive parrot, paired with dual daggers that scream "I'm here to party, but also to stab." Her dynamic pose suggests she's mid-dance or mid-pounce, fitting for a Rogue. Compared to the grimdark warriors usually found in the game, she brings a vibrant, almost playful energy—until you realize those daggers are bewitched. It's a great design that clearly communicates her agility and her family origin.
In the current landscape of 2026, Alessia's stats are beginning to show their age. With a base Power of 488 and stats hovering around
, she is noticeably behind modern 3-star juggernauts.

. She is squishier than the current standard, meaning she relies heavily on her Rogue talent (Dodge) and Limit Breaks to survive long enough to fire. Without double Limit Breaks, she risks being one-shot by modern snipers.
is the core of Alessia's kit, and it’s a skill that punches above its weight class due to its
Dealing 250% damage to three targets at Fast speed is excellent coverage for a 3-star. It softens up the enemy lines for a finisher.
The 204 damage (over 3 turns) to Max HP is the star of the show. While 68 damage per turn doesn't sound like a lot, it’s permanent damage that cannot be healed back by ordinary means.
If the target has boosted health, the effect is stronger. This makes her a direct counter to Faiez and other overhealers who dominate Rush tournaments.
the initial hit. This is important because she doesn't reduce the ceiling before hitting, but rather hits and then starts the shrinking process.
Fast (8 tiles). With a Lv. 11 Mana Troop or Lv. 5 Magic/Styx Troop + Emblem node, she can become functionally very fast in 3-star tournaments (where troops are limited).
204 x 3 targets = 612 total Max HP reduction.
~1362% effective damage value spread across 3 targets over 3 turns.
By reducing Max HP, she effectively "deals damage" that healers like Belith or Hawkmoon cannot reverse.
The cleanse on minion death is highly situational since she doesn't summon minions herself. It relies on teammates (like Dawn or Kvasir) or enemy minion destroyers.
Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
family and her passive kit are a mixed bag of utility.
Adds Attack buffs or Ailment Immunity to the minion owner.
This is huge. Starting the battle with a "meat shield" effectively buffs her HP pool, helping mitigate her lower base stats. If you run her with another family member (like
if allowed in 4-star mix), the attack boost is a nice cherry on top.
She cleanses ailments when her minion dies.

This pairs perfectly with her family bonus. She starts with a minion; if an enemy sniper kills it, she instantly cleanses any ailments (like defense down) she might have picked up. It's a clever defensive fail-safe.
Grants +20% Attack for the first 6 turns.
Simple and effective. It boosts her initial Bewitched Daggers strike, helping her secure kills early in the match before the battle drags on.
Upon release, Alessia was a top-tier pick for Bloody Battle and Rush tournaments. She offered a rare mechanic (Max HP reduction) to the 3-star arena, previously dominated by simple raw damage.
Her stats have fallen behind. Modern 3-stars have nearly 200 more HP and 50+ more Defense. However,
remains a premium and rare mechanic in 3-star rarity.
. She is no longer a "put in every team" hero. She shines specifically in

(where healing is disabled, but Max HP reduction still works to lower the kill threshold) and against heavy boosting teams involving Faiez. In standard raid formats, she is too fragile compared to newer options like
Alessia is a Rogue, which allows her to evade direct damage from special skills. This is crucial for her survival.
Maximizes the 250% hit. Since she is Fast, you want that hit to hurt. The Rogue talent helps her dodge, so you rely on RNG for survival rather than raw HP.
She remains glassy. If the dodge fails, she dies.
Increases the durability of her starting Minion (since minion HP comes from owner max HP). Helps her survive slash attacks.
Her special skill hits like a wet noodle, relying entirely on the Max HP reduction utility.

path is recommended. Alessia's job is to disrupt and damage quickly. If she fires once or twice with high attack, she has done her job. If you play defensively, she might survive longer but won't impact the board enough to win.
Max Health reduction is functionally unhealable damage in 3-star tournaments (unless they have their own max HP booster).
She charges in 8 tiles (or fewer with mana support), allowing her to dictate the pace.
The family bonus gives her an immediate buffer against early tile damage.
The evade chance can single-handedly win games by dodging a lethal sniper shot.
Her base survivability is low by 2026 standards.
A significant portion of her "damage" is the HP reduction over 3 turns. If the enemy cleanses or the battle ends quickly, she loses value.
The 204 HP reduction is a fixed number. As 3-star HP pools grow larger (hitting 1300+ with troops/LB2), 204 becomes a smaller percentage of the total.
Her absolute best mode. Healers are banned, but her Max HP reduction acts as a pseudo-DoT that brings enemies into kill range faster.
Her starting minion counts as a buff/effect, and if paired with other family members, she gains more buffs.
Viable as a counter to Faiez, punishing his boosted health.
A fast/very fast green stack. Alessia softens three targets, and Zarola (who needs full mana enemies or quick kills) or Noril cleans up.
Alessia punishes the enemy Faiez's boost, while your own Faiez protects the team. Waqas adds more max HP reduction for a "strangle" strategy.
(like Helo or cTyrum) remove the Max HP reduction DoT before it can fully tick down.

Don't pair her with heroes that block status effects if it interferes with her reduction, though that is rare in 3-star.
. A fast, hit-3 hero is invaluable for clearing map stages and early raid tiers. Her minion gives her extra durability that new players will appreciate when they lack healers.

for tournament enthusiasts. If you care about ranking top 1% in 3-star tournaments, she is worth keeping and potentially Limit Breaking once. However, think twice before committing Alpha Aethers (LB2) unless you absolutely love her playstyle; newer heroes offer better stats for the investment.
The community reaction to Alessia was overwhelmingly positive upon release, with many comparing her favorably to 5-star heroes.
Players loved that she brought a 5-star mechanic (Horus's max HP reduction) to the 3-star level.
There was a debate on whether she was better than Zarola. One user noted:
"Alessia even with her crappy HP reduction is already better than the currently best hero in 3 stars fast damage department
citing her Rogue class as the tiebreaker.
Some players critiqued that the HP reduction happens
"Would be better if it happened before the damage... Damage then hp reduction is underwhelming."
Alessia is like a masquerade dancer who brings a knife to a fistfight—stylish, quick, and dangerous. While Father Time has eroded her stats, her
remain a potent tool in the specific sandbox of 3-star tournaments. She isn't the raw powerhouse she was in 2023, but in a Bloody Battle, she’s still one of the best ways to bleed your opponent dry.
A solid B+ hero in the modern era, rising to an A in Bloody Battle tournaments. Keep one copy, mask and all.

A comprehensive review and strategy guide for Alessia, the 3-star Nature Rogue from the Carnival of Gods. We analyze her fast-speed max health reduction, stats, and viability in the current Empires & Puzzles meta.
